steps.title: Mark the interrupting phrase
steps.content: The phrase according to Swentzell interrupts the subject and verb of the sentence. Since a comma already appears before according, the phrase needs a matching comma after Swentzell.

steps.title: Reject mismatched punctuation
steps.content: A leaves the interrupting phrase unclosed. C uses a colon even though the preceding words do not form an independent clause. D uses a dash that does not match the opening comma. The needed punctuation is a closing comma. The answer is B.
